Landing.title: Who's the Undercover · AI Table
Landing.tagline: A table of distinct AI players, each holding a word — one of them is the undercover, and none of them know who. Watch them describe, probe, and accuse — or take a seat yourself, or sit a masterclass.
Landing.modeSpectate: Spectate — watch the AIs play and bet on the undercover.
Landing.modePlay: Sit in — take a seat and play alongside the AIs.
Landing.modeMasterclass: Masterclass — you play with a coach who guides you, from the basics up.

Play.tagline: Several AI players each hold a word — one of them is the undercover, and none of them know who. Watch them describe, probe, and accuse — or take a seat at the table yourself.
Play.footer: Every AI's clue and vote is generated live by an AI / local model · same word, a different game every time

Setup.title: New game
Setup.desc: Set the players, the number of spies, and the words, then watch the AIs probe each other. Want to play yourself? Pick "Sit in". Want to be coached while you play? Pick "🎓 Masterclass".
Setup.modeSpectateTip: Just watch the AIs play; you can bet on who the undercover is
Setup.modePlayTip: Take a seat and play alongside the AIs
Setup.modeMasterclassTip: You play, with a coach who guides you step by step, from simple to deep — to sharpen your game
Setup.blankTip: Add 1 blank: they get no word and must bluff from others' clues
Setup.devModeTip: After every statement, all AIs re-score how much they suspect each player (heavier API usage)
Setup.advancedTip: Tune each AI's name / persona / thinking style / model / attributes individually

Board.humanSpyGuess: 🔪 You're the last spy! Guess the civilians' word to steal the win — type your guess:
Board.humanBlank: 🃏 You're the blank (no word)! Guess what everyone means from their clues and drop a vague line without giving yourself away.
Board.humanDescribe: Your turn · your word is 【{word}】 — describe it in one line (don't say the word)
Board.placeholderGuess: Your guess for the civilians' word…
Board.humanVotePk: Tie-break revote · pick one of the tied players
Board.humanVote: Your turn to vote · who's the undercover?
Board.guessHintEnded: Game over — see if you guessed right:
Board.guessHintActive: Who's the undercover? Tap a name to bet.
Board.guessLocked: 🔒 Bet locked — now sit back and watch it play out.
Board.guessClosed: Betting is closed for this game (locked after round 1).
Board.verdictRight: 🎯 You got it! The undercover was {name}
Board.inProgress: Game in progress. A panel will appear below when it's your turn.

GameLog.gameStartBlank: Game on · {players} players are seated, with {spies} undercover hidden among them, plus 1 blank (no word).
GameLog.gameStartNoBlank: Game on · {players} players are seated, with {spies} undercover hidden among them.
GameLog.gameIntro: Civilians and the undercover each got a similar-but-different word. None of them know yet who the odd one out is.
GameLog.reflecting: The AIs are reviewing the game and noting lessons…
GameLog.reflectDone: Review done — {total} lessons written to the AIs' long-term memory (carried into the next game).
GameLog.spyComebackPrompt: {name} is the last undercover — one last-chance guess: name the civilians' word to steal the win.
GameLog.tieBreak: It's a tie ({names}) — PK round: each adds one more clue, then a revote.
GameLog.pkStillTie: Still tied after the PK — no one is eliminated this round.
GameLog.elimSpy: {name} is voted out — and was the undercover!
GameLog.elimBlank: {name} is voted out, but was the blank (no word at all)! The undercover is still in play…
GameLog.elimCiv: {name} is voted out, but was a civilian. The undercover is still in play…
GameLog.resultCiv: 🎉 The civilians win! The undercover has been caught.
GameLog.errLazy: You can't say things like "same as above" — give a real one-line description.
GameLog.errDup: You can't repeat someone else's clue word-for-word — try a different angle.
GameLog.errLeak: Your clue can't contain your own word — say it another way.
GameLog.errDescribe: Describe phase error: {msg} (click "Start this round" to retry).

Tutorial.skipTip: Skip the intro and start the game
Tutorial.card0Body: Most players are civilians holding the same word; a few undercover players hold a similar-but-different word. At the start, nobody knows whether they're a civilian or the undercover — you work it out from what people say. (Advanced games also have a blank, who gets no word at all.)
Tutorial.card1Body: Each round, everyone describes their word in one line — without saying the word itself. After everyone has spoken, you vote, and whoever gets the most votes is eliminated and revealed.
Tutorial.card2Body: If you're a civilian: work with the others to vote out the undercover. If you're the undercover: stay hidden and survive to the end; even if you're voted out, you can still steal the win by guessing the civilians' word.
Tutorial.card3Body: Don't describe too directly (you'll expose yourself) or too vaguely (you'll look suspicious). When voting, find the person whose description 'doesn't quite fit the rest' — that's likely the undercover.
Tutorial.card4Body: You're at a table with a few AIs — but you won't see their private thoughts, and that's the point. Your coach (above the table) reads the clues for you and guides you, gently at first and deeper as the game goes on, so you learn to read the table yourself. When it's your turn, follow the coach. Ready? Let's go!
Tutorial.hintEndBody: The undercover was {name} (word: '{word}'). Look back at everyone's clues: did the undercover's description not quite fit? You can play again to keep practising, or leave the masterclass to play for real.
Tutorial.hintDescribeBody: Your word is 【{word}】. Describe a feature of it (look / use / scene) in one line — without saying the word itself, and not too obviously — then send it below.
Tutorial.hintVoteBody: Recall everyone's clues (and your coach's read), and vote for the person who 'fits least' — the most suspicious one. Getting it wrong is fine; this is practice.
Tutorial.hintReadyBody: Click 'Start this round'. Each player (including the AIs) speaks in turn — read each clue as it lands, and let your coach point out what matters.
Tutorial.hintDescribingBodyHuman: Read each AI's clue as it lands and notice whose description doesn't fit the majority — your coach will help you spot it.
Tutorial.hintDescribedBody: Click 'Go to vote'. Before you vote, think: whose description was most off?
Tutorial.hintVotingBody: Watch who votes for whom, and why.
Tutorial.hintRevealedBody: The eliminated player's identity is revealed above. If it's not decided yet, click 'Next round' to continue.

Stats.title: 📊 Your record
Stats.empty: No record yet. After a game (a spectator bet or a seat at the table), your win rate, streaks, and achievements show up here.
Stats.ach_first_win_desc: Win for the first time (you win, or correctly bet the undercover)
Stats.ach_both_sides_desc: Witness a win for both the civilians and the undercover

Memory.title: 🧠 AI memory (independent)
Memory.empty: No memories yet. After a game, each AI builds up lessons and recalls them next game.
Memory.recalled: Recalled lessons ({n} at this game's start)

Suspicion.title: 🔬 Live suspicion matrix
Suspicion.intro: After every statement, each AI re-scores how much it suspects everyone else (0–100). This updates live as clues land.

AISlot.name: name
AISlot.attributes: Attributes (injected into the prompt as persona; never used by code to decide for it)
